verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 (Cysteine-Rich Secretory Protein 1, also known as AEG1/DE/CRISP-1) is a secreted protein of the CRISP/Pr-1/Pathogenesis-related protein 1 (CAP) superfamily. It lacks a transmembrane domain and is secreted by the epididymis into the epididymal lumen, where it becomes stably associated with the sperm plasma membrane surface — specifically the head and tail. This association is wash-resistant in physiological conditions and mediates sperm-egg fusion and ion channel regulation on sperm. The protein is detected on the outer face of the sperm plasma membrane. However, this surface display is strictly tissue/cell-type restricted to sperm and epididymal context. It does not appear on somatic cell surfaces at baseline. This fits 'contextual' / 'tissue_restricted_surface': the protein reaches the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane in a narrow germline/reproductive lineage context via stable non-covalent (or partly covalent) association with the sperm surface, but is absent from the surface of other cell types.
predicted_key_uncertainty: The exact biochemical mechanism anchoring CRISP1 to the sperm PM (wash-resistance level, whether covalent or non-covalent GPI-linked or lipid-raft-associated) is not fully characterized, affecting whether it meets stable_surface_attachment vs tissue_restricted_surface.
